description: Circle Summary → CCB: when a leader adds new people via "Add to roster", those pending names are now also counted as guests on the CCB event summary. So 7 checked roster attendees + 2 pending adds shows as 9 total attendance in CCB (7 attendees + 2 guests).

Circle Summary events page: cached events list now persists across browser sessions so repeat visits paint instantly, and the loading state shows "Loading your Circle events…" — escalating to "Still loading — pulling the latest from Church Community Builder" after 5 seconds so leaders know the page isn't stuck.

Circle Summary → CCB: the notes pushed to CCB now preserve the line breaks leaders type (paragraph breaks, lists) instead of being flattened into one line. Dynamic question answers each appear on their own block with the question as a heading; the "Additional questions —" prefix is gone.

Circle Summary: "Add someone to my Circle" requests are now appended to the bottom of the notes pushed to CCB (one blank line before each block, format: "Add New Person to Roster:" then name, phone, email on their own lines) — making it easy for ACPDs to spot and process them. The pending entries no longer go through a separate roster-request flow.

Circle Summary attendance: pending "add to roster" requests now have always-visible Edit and Remove actions on each row, so leaders can fix a typo or remove someone they added by mistake before submitting.

Circle Summary → CCB push: literal \n\n characters no longer appear in Event Notes when the leader includes line breaks in dynamic question responses, the topic, prayer requests, or info fields. All text headed to CCB is now flattened to a single line with " || " between paragraph breaks.
Settings → Circle Summary: removed the Submission Log and Info Update Requests tiles. Those admin pages have been retired.

Circle Summary attendance: roster removal is now behind an Edit toggle — tap Edit to reveal a red minus on each removable person, with a clear instruction line warning that the action removes them from the Circle's roster. Tap Done to return to taking attendance.
Circle Summary Thank You page now uses leader-friendly language ("Your Circle summary has been submitted.") and no longer shows an internal reference code.
Circle Summary sign-in now asks for email only — phone number is no longer offered as an identifier, simplifying the leader login flow.
Circle Summary: removed the "VC" emblem from the sign-in hero for a cleaner header.
Circle Summary: the SMS sent from the admin magic-link tool now reads as friendly end-user copy — "here's your link to report your Circle event summary" — instead of the technical sign-in phrasing.
Boards: hover a card and press F to toggle its focus state — same as clicking 'Set as Focus' in the card detail, but without opening the card.

Circle Summary loads noticeably faster: the events page paints the header instantly while CCB data loads in the background, CCB results are cached for 60 seconds, and the events list is remembered so navigating back from an event is near-instant. The sign-in page no longer flashes for already-signed-in leaders.
Fixed odd characters (literal \n\n) appearing in CCB Event Notes when a Circle Summary included line breaks. Paragraph breaks are now displayed cleanly as " || " separators.
Circle Summary form now shows only dynamic questions with no extra static fields. Inputs and radio buttons have a more visible border for easier tapping and typing.

Circle Leaders can now remove someone from their roster on the Circle Summary attendance page. The Remove action only takes the person out of the Circle's CCB group — their CCB profile is not changed.
Circle Summary admin pages (Dynamic Questions, Submission Log, Info Update Requests) are now linked from a dedicated Circle Summary tab in Settings.

Circle Summary form now shows only the questions configured in Dynamic Questions — no default fields. The admin page shows a warning when no active questions are configured, status badges (Always / Active / Upcoming / Expired) on each question, and a clear Visibility toggle when creating or editing a question.

Circle Summary magic links now reliably sign leaders in. The auth handler sets the session cookie on a 200 page and then navigates, avoiding a hosting quirk that was dropping the cookie on the redirect and bouncing leaders back to the sign-in screen.
Choosing a primary leader from CCB on Circle forms now also fills in that person's CCB profile link automatically, so you don't have to copy the individual URL by hand.

Admin "send magic link" now builds the link using the host you're currently on, so leaders aren't bounced through a cross-domain redirect that strips their session cookie. Tapping the link signs them straight into Circle Summary as intended.

"Send magic link" on a Circle page now shows a clear error if the request fails (instead of crashing with "Unexpected end of JSON input"), and the server returns the actual error message instead of a bare 500.
Circle Summary reflection step is now a single open-ended prompt — "Tell us about your Circle" — instead of four separate fields. Less friction, more room for whatever the leader wants to share.

Circle Summary email reminders: turn on per-leader from the Quick Actions panel and they'll get a VCC-branded, mobile-friendly email an hour before each meeting plus a follow-up every morning until they submit. Each email has a one-tap link that signs them in automatically — no password.

On a Circle Leader profile, the Event Summary panel now has a "Text Circle Summary link" button. Tap it and your phone's texting app opens with a pre-filled message containing a magic sign-in link addressed to the leader — perfect for nudging a leader from your phone after Circle.

Circle Summary URLs now include the leader's CCB group ID (e.g. /circle-summary/12345/events) so you can tell which Circle you're looking at. The events list and submission form always show the logged-in leader's own Circle — manually changing the group ID in the URL redirects you back to your own Circle, and all data still comes from the session, so other Circles' data can't be reached.

Auto-Update from CCB no longer marks circles as "Did Not Meet" if their meeting is scheduled for today or later in the week. CCB pre-creates attendance records before meetings happen and defaults them to Did Not Meet — the auto-update now skips those until the meeting date has passed.

Circle Summary is live: leaders sign in with a 6-digit code emailed to them, see their last 8 weeks of Circle meetings, and submit topic, notes, prayer requests, attendees, and follow-up items in one tap. Submissions push straight into CCB and email the ACPD automatically. Editing a previously submitted summary pre-fills the form so corrections don't need to be retyped.

ACPD admin pages for Circle Summary: build dynamic questions that appear on the leader form (with date windows and field types), review meeting-info change requests with one click to apply them to the leader's profile, and audit every submission with CCB push status.
Circle Summary 'Did your Circle meet?' toggle now reliably renders as a green pill switch when on, instead of falling back to a default checkbox in some browsers.

Notebook pages now support Ink mode: flip an individual page from rich text to a drawing surface, write with Apple Pencil or any pointer device, pinch or wheel-zoom, erase whole strokes, and keep text and ink saved independently.

Notebook feels dramatically faster: pages you've already visited open instantly from an in-memory cache, switching pages no longer triggers a blank spinner, the root /notebook redirect no longer double-fetches your folders, the heavy rich-text editor and link pickers now load on-demand, and every loading state shows a content-shaped skeleton instead of a lonely spinner.

Notebook page editor reworked on iPad — the title is now an auto-growing textarea (no more text getting scrolled or clipped inside an input element), padding from the sidebar scales generously across tablet breakpoints (56px on iPad portrait, 107px on landscape), the title font scales up at md and uses the page font family, and the title's bottom margin is restored so it visually separates from the body.

Notebook on iPad now adapts to orientation — in portrait the right-side links panel tucks away as a bottom sheet so the typing area gets full width, and rotating to landscape docks the panel back alongside the editor.

Auto-update from CCB now matches leaders even when CCB titles include a co-leader name (e.g. "FMT | S2 | Shannon and Sherrie Hawk") by checking that both the leader's first and last name appear in the event title — a huge bump in how many summaries get pulled in a single click.

Auto-update from CCB now falls back to matching by the leader's name when their stored CCB group name doesn't quite match the event title in CCB — so leaders whose group name in RADIUS includes extra detail finally get their summaries pulled.

The CCB Event Explorer no longer silently shows "No events found" when CCB is actually rejecting the request — credential and service-permission errors from CCB now surface with the real error message so problems can be diagnosed instead of guessed at.
The floating quick-actions menu is simpler now — the extra Start a New Note shortcut has been removed so the list stays focused on the actions you actually use in the moment.
Prayer points on the Prayer page now keep separate Pray on and Prayed on dates, so you can schedule when to pray and also log when you actually prayed without overwriting either one.
Notebook folder icons now use a categorized Lucide icon picker instead of emoji, with selected icons shown directly in the sidebar for a cleaner, more consistent UI.
Checklist suggestions now let you choose whether to add items to an existing checklist or start a new one — no more duplicate checklists.
AI Summarize now inserts properly formatted content — headers, bullet lists, and line breaks are preserved as rich text instead of collapsing into a wall of plain text.
Board cards now have AI checklist suggestions — tap "Suggest" in the Checklists section to get AI-generated next steps and open items pulled from the card's description.
Board card descriptions now support voice dictation and AI summarize — tap the mic to speak your notes directly into the description field, or use AI Summarize to clean them up.
URLs in description fields (boards, notes) are now automatically rendered as clickable links — no need to manually insert a link with the toolbar.

Paste Import on the Bulk Message page — paste a raw First / Last / Phone list, review and edit parsed entries inline, then add them to your recipient list in one click. Pasted entries are saved with the rest of your list using the existing "Save as List" flow.

New Copy for CCB shortcut on the Notes page — click the clipboard icon on any note to copy it as plain text and open the leader's CCB profile in one click, ready to paste. Or use "Save + Copy for CCB" while composing to save and copy in one step.
Notebook sidebar now updates immediately when pages are deleted or changed, so removed pages disappear right away and newly available pages reliably show up without needing a refresh.

Notebook AI checklists: use Suggest in the Checklists panel to read the current note and propose concise next-step or outstanding-item checklist entries, with source context shown underneath each item.
Notebook-linked cards now support AI checklist suggestions too — open a linked card from the Notebook page, use Suggest in the card checklist area, and add selected items directly to the card.

Saved checklist items now use a cleaner two-line layout: a short item title with smaller supporting context beneath it. AI-created checklist items preserve their source note line as the supporting detail.
Creating a card from a Notebook page now defaults the card title to the note title, keeps it editable before saving, and adds a backlink to the original note in the card description.
New Notebooks page: organize your notes in one place with folders, page navigation, and a focused writing workspace for ongoing leader conversations and follow-up planning.
Added subtle left padding to the Notebook page title field so title text starts with better spacing and aligns more cleanly with editor content.
Cleaned up Notebook checklist controls by moving bulk checklist actions into a compact actions menu, reducing visual clutter while keeping Mark all complete, Clear completed, and Duplicate available.
Refined Notebook checklist readability by adding left padding inside checklist title and item text fields and reducing checklist text size for a cleaner, less crowded look.

Notebook checklists now default to "Checklist" when you create one, long checklist item text wraps fully, and each checklist includes quick bulk actions to Mark all complete, Clear completed, or Duplicate.
Fixed Add Leader reliability and cleaned up duplicate Status options in the Add Leader form so each status now appears once and leader creation uses the secured API flow.

Notebook pages can now be moved and reordered by dragging in the sidebar — hover a page to reveal the drag handle, then drag within a folder or into a different folder. Order and folder placement are saved automatically.
Mass Update now supports Status — you can bulk-change the status (invited, on-boarding, active, paused, off-boarding) for multiple circle leaders at once from the Import & Manage Circles page.

Host Team Leaders — Track volunteer team leaders (ushers, kids, hospitality, etc.) alongside Circle Leaders using the same relationship tools. Add Host Team Leaders via the Add Leader form, filter by type on the Find Leaders page, and manage HT Directors in Settings.

Unified button and color styling across the entire app — all pages and modals now use the same consistent design system instead of a mix of blue, green, purple, and teal variants. The Bulk Message iMessage bubble and START BATCH button are intentionally kept in iMessage blue.
Fixed a touch dead zone on the Events calendar — the Quick Actions FAB was invisibly blocking taps on the Summary, Reminder, Profile, and CCB buttons when the FAB was closed.
Backend security hardening: moved CCB API credentials out of source code into environment variables, and added admin-only protection to the campus creation endpoint.
Performance improvements across the app — reduced unnecessary re-renders in the auth layer, sped up board data loading, and reduced the amount of data fetched when loading circle leader notes.
When turning on a follow-up from the circle profile, the generated Notes timeline entry now includes the full follow-up message you typed (not just the date).
Circle follow-up quick action now switches labels based on status: Turn On when follow-up is off, and Turn Off when it's active.
Follow-up and profile updates now consistently log to the circle leader Notes timeline when you save edits, including follow-up note/date changes.
Circle follow-up quick actions now use clearer labels: the top action says Turn Off and the date-row action says Edit.
Confirmation dialogs now center and evenly spread action buttons across the modal footer for clearer button layout on follow-up and other confirm prompts.
Circle Quick Actions follow-up now shows an Edit button (instead of the Required badge), and the follow-up modal lets you update both date and note in one place.
Circle follow-up cards were cleaned up so the Change Date action sits near the date at the top, and the temporary 'Due Soon' status message was removed for a simpler quick actions layout.
Circle profile follow-ups now show the saved follow-up note in Quick Actions, and follow-up notes added from the Quick Actions + menu are automatically written to the leader's Notes timeline.
Boards: Click any column's color dot to change it from a 12-color palette. New columns also automatically get a unique color so no two columns look the same.
Boards: The 'Add Column' button now matches the Tools button style in the nav — consistent frosted glass look across the app.
When importing circle leaders to a board, the auto-generated card description now puts each detail (campus, circle type, day, time, phone, email) on its own line for easier reading.
Board descriptions now show tighter contact chips, and matching call, text, and email actions also appear on board comments and circle notes when contact info is mentioned.
Board card descriptions now surface phone numbers and email addresses as one-tap contact actions so you can call, text, or email directly from the card.

Circle leader pages now show a birthday banner when a leader's (or additional leader's) birthday is within the next 7 days — highlighted in violet alongside the existing prayer and encouragement banners.
The Add Board Card modal now remembers the last board and list you chose, so you don't have to re-select them every time. Cards also support an optional description field for adding context or notes.
Prayer requests can now have a scheduled date — they'll appear on your Today page and in the daily digest email on the day you want to pray for them.
Boards buttons (New Board, view toggle) now use the same subtle style as the Tools nav button — consistent look across the app.
The Tools button in the top nav now shows a label and dropdown arrow — no more mystery icon.
Quick Actions FAB: Tap the + button in the bottom-left corner to instantly log a note, connection, or prayer, set a follow-up, or add a board card — from anywhere in the app.
Boards: You can now sort your boards by Newest, A–Z, Last Updated, or a Custom drag-and-drop order. Your preference is saved automatically.
Prayer Page: Leader names are now clickable links that take you directly to their profile.
Added Update Log to the account menu (below Help) so you can always see what's changed in RADIUS.
Bulk Message: You can now search for and manually add individual leaders to a message batch — even ones that don't match your current filters. Search by name, phone, or campus and tap to add.
Bulk Message: Added keyboard shortcuts during a batch — press Space or Enter to open Messages, press S to skip. Shortcut hints are shown on the buttons.
Bulk Message: You can now pause a batch mid-way and resume right where you left off instead of starting over.
